dictionaryVocabulary.description: Formal or polite morning greeting, especially with teachers, coworkers, or strangers.

example.japanese: 先生におはようございますと言います。
example.kana: せんせいにおはようございますといいます。
example.romaji: Sensei ni ohayou gozaimasu to iimasu.
example.english: I say good morning to the teacher.

dictionaryVocabulary.description: A standard daytime greeting that is safe in many situations.

example.japanese: 友だちにこんにちはと言います。
example.kana: ともだちにこんにちはといいます。
example.english: I say hello to my friend.

example.japanese: 夜にこんばんはと言います。
example.kana: よるにこんばんはといいます。
example.english: At night, I say good evening.

dictionaryVocabulary.description: Used the first time you meet someone.

example.japanese: はじめまして。キムです。
example.kana: はじめまして。きむです。
example.english: Nice to meet you. I am Kim.

dictionaryVocabulary.description: A formal goodbye that often suggests a longer separation.

example.japanese: 先生にさようならと言います。
example.kana: せんせいにさようならといいます。
example.english: I say goodbye to the teacher.

dictionaryVocabulary.description: A friendly casual goodbye used with people you know.

example.japanese: 友だちにまたねと言います。
example.kana: ともだちにまたねといいます。
example.romaji: Tomodachi ni mata ne to iimasu.
example.english: I say see you to my friend.

exercises.title: Greetings & Farewells — Formal · Casual · Slang
exercises.intro: Japanese greetings change dramatically by register. The same 'good morning' is おはようございます at work, おはよう with friends, and おっす in youth slang. Learn the three side-by-side so your default becomes situational, not mechanical.
rows: Good morning — before ~10 am; to superiors or strangers
Hello / Good afternoon — midday greeting, all contexts
はじめまして。よろしくおねがいします。
Nice to meet you — first meeting only
よろしくおねがいします
こちらこそ、よろしくおねがいします。
Please treat me well — requesting goodwill
How are you? (formal) — best when re-meeting after a gap
Good night (formal) — parting at night or before sleep
Morning — drop ございます with friends and classmates
ほんとうだね！ひさしぶり！
Long time no see! — for friends you haven't seen in a while
Nice to meet you (casual) / Nice to have you on board
Bye then — slightly warmer than またね
Good night (casual) — drop なさい with close friends
Yo! — very casual, mainly male youth
Hi! — short form of こんにちは; widely used online
Bye bye — borrowed from English, all ages
See ya — very casual またね; mainly male
exercises.takeaway: Start FORMAL when in doubt. Drop honorifics only once you know the relationship.

lines.speaker: やまもと
lines.jp: はじめまして。やまもとと もうします。どうぞよろしくおねがいします。
lines.romaji: Hajimemashite. Yamamoto to moushimasu. Douzo yoroshiku onegaishimasu.
lines.en: Nice to meet you. My name is Yamamoto. Please treat me well.

lines.speaker: キム
lines.jp: はじめまして。キムです。こちらこそ、どうぞよろしくおねがいします。
lines.romaji: Hajimemashite. Kimu desu. Kochira koso, douzo yoroshiku onegaishimasu.
lines.en: Nice to meet you. I'm Kim. Likewise, please treat me well.

lines.speaker: やまもと
lines.jp: それでは、またあした。おげんきで。
lines.en: Well then, see you tomorrow. Take care.

lines.speaker: そうた
lines.jp: ひさしぶり！ げんき？
lines.en: Long time no see! You good?

lines.speaker: みか
lines.jp: うん、げんき！ そうたくんは？
lines.en: Yeah, I'm good! And you, Souta?

lines.speaker: はる
lines.jp: よ！ ゆー、ひさしぶりじゃん！

lines.speaker: ゆー
lines.jp: そうそう！ またな、ばいばい！
lines.romaji: Sou sou! Mata na, bai bai!

exercises.prompt: Which morning greeting fits a business meeting?
exercises.title: Morning greeting

variants.label: FORMAL
variants.note: To teachers, bosses, strangers. Always safe in formal contexts.

variants.label: CASUAL
variants.note: Drop ございます with friends and family.

variants.label: SLANG
variants.note: Very compressed greeting; mainly used by males.

exercises.takeaway: Formality in Japanese is word choice — pick the register that matches the situation.

exercises.prompt: Which self-introduction is the standard N5 default?
exercises.title: Introducing yourself for the first time

variants.label: FORMAL
variants.jp: わたしは やまもとと もうします。
variants.note: Business / formal situations. Humble form of 'I am called'.

variants.label: STANDARD
variants.jp: わたしは やまもとです。
variants.note: The standard N5 introduction pattern — use this as your default.

variants.label: CASUAL
variants.note: Drop わたしは and です with close friends.

exercises.takeaway: わたしは [NAME]です is the N5 default — [NAME]と もうします is formal; [NAME]だよ is casual.
exercises.speakText: わたしは やまもとです。

exercises.prompt: How would you ask a teacher 'how are you?'
exercises.title: Asking 'how are you?'

variants.label: FORMAL
variants.note: Reply: はい、おかげさまで。 'Yes, I'm well, thank you.'

variants.label: CASUAL
variants.note: Reply: うん、げんき！ 'Yeah, I'm good!' — friends only.

exercises.takeaway: With teachers and superiors, use おげんきですか？ and reply with おかげさまで。

exercises.prompt: Which goodbye fits saying bye to classmates?
exercises.title: Saying goodbye

variants.label: FORMAL
variants.note: Formal; carries a sense of finality. Less common in daily life.

variants.label: CASUAL
variants.note: The everyday casual goodbye — universally understood.

variants.label: SLANG
variants.note: Borrowed from English; all ages informally — but too casual for class.

exercises.takeaway: さようなら sounds like a final farewell — use またね / じゃあね for everyday goodbyes.

exercises.prompt: Introduce yourself formally as Tanaka:

slot.hint: The family name 'Tanaka' in Japanese script.

exercises.translation: Nice to meet you. My name is Tanaka.
exercises.speakText: はじめまして。たなかと もうします。

exercises.prompt: Give the standard N5 self-introduction as Tanaka:

slot.hint: The family name 'Tanaka' in Japanese script.

exercises.speakText: わたしは たなかです。

exercises.prompt: Bounce the question back to Souta (casual):

slot.hint: Souta's name plus the boy-friendly honorific -kun.

exercises.translation: Yeah, I'm good! And you, Souta?
exercises.speakText: うん、げんき！ そうたくんは？

cards.back: Hajimemashite — Nice to meet you (first meeting)

cards.front: よろしくおねがいします
cards.back: Yoroshiku onegaishimasu — Please treat me well / Pleased to meet you
cards.speakText: よろしくおねがいします

cards.back: Ogenki desu ka? — How are you? (formal)
Okage sama de — I'm well, thank you (set phrase)
Kochira koso — Likewise / Same to you

pairs.left: よろしくおねがいします

cards.jp: はじめまして。やまもとと もうします。どうぞよろしくおねがいします。
cards.romaji: Hajimemashite. Yamamoto to moushimasu. Douzo yoroshiku onegaishimasu.
cards.en: Nice to meet you. My name is Yamamoto. Please treat me well.

cards.jp: はじめまして。キムです。こちらこそ、どうぞよろしくおねがいします。
cards.romaji: Hajimemashite. Kimu desu. Kochira koso, douzo yoroshiku onegaishimasu.
cards.en: Nice to meet you. I'm Kim. Likewise, please treat me well.

cards.jp: それでは、またあした。おげんきで。
cards.en: Well then, see you tomorrow. Take care.

cards.jp: おー、そうたくん、ひさしぶり！げんき？
cards.en: Oh, Souta! Long time no see! How are you?

cards.jp: うん、げんき！ みかちゃんは？
おっす、はる！ひさしぶりやん！げんき？
cards.en: Yo, Haru! It's been ages! You good?

cards.jp: おっす！めっちゃげんき、ゆーは？
そうそう！ またな、ばいばい！
cards.romaji: Sou sou! Mata na, bai bai!

cards.jp: こちらこそ、よろしくおねがいします。
